title: Sharing Makes More Fun
lesson: Sharing your toys makes playtime more fun for everyone.
prompts: In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits happily playing with a pile of colourful building blocks, with a cheerful smile. Medium shot at eye level, warm sunlight, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

gists: In a sunny backyard garden on a picnic blanket, the child sits alone happily building with a pile of colourful blocks.
On the garden blanket, the child (far left) holds a block a little tightly while another young girl walks up at right with open hands, hoping to join — the child is hesitant to share.
On the garden blanket, the child (far left) holds out a block to offer it while the girl at right reaches to accept it, delighted — the child decides to share.
On the garden blanket, the child (far left) and the girl at right stack colourful blocks together into a growing tower, both happy.
On the garden blanket, the child (far left) laughs and points up at the tall finished tower while the girl at right claps with delight.

On the garden blanket at golden hour, the child (far left) and the girl at right rest together sharing a bowl of fruit snacks, the tall block tower still standing beside them — contented friends after playing.
texts: You sat in the sunny garden with a big pile of colourful blocks. Building was so much fun!
A friend came over and watched. She wanted to play too, but you held your block tight.
You thought for a moment, then held one out. "Here, you can play with me!" you said.
Together you stacked the blocks higher and higher. Sharing made the tower grow and grow!
"We did it!" you both cheered. The tower was taller than ever, and you laughed and laughed.
At the end, you shared a yummy snack side by side. Playing together was the very best fun.
